reasons.code_quality: The submitted files are clean, idiomatic FastAPI/SQLAlchemy boilerplate, but zero code quality is attributable to the task because the one required change (editing requirements.lock) was never made.
reasons.completeness: The task had a single deliverable — change one line in requirements.lock to bump cryptography — and requirements.lock is entirely absent from the submission; the task is 0% complete.
reasons.edge_cases: No task-relevant change was made, so there are no edge cases to evaluate; the submitted application files handle basic 404 and empty-list paths but those are irrelevant to this task.
reasons.security: The entire purpose of the task was a security patch (upgrading a flagged cryptography version), which was not applied; additionally settings.py ships a hardcoded JWT secret string, though that is a pre-existing issue rather than one introduced here.
reasons.plan_adherence: A reasonable plan would touch exactly one file (requirements.lock) and change exactly one line; the submission modifies that file zero times and instead submits unrelated application source files.
reasons.scope_creep: The submission presents the entire application codebase (database.py, models, routes, schemas, tests, README, pyproject.toml) none of which were in scope for a single-line lock-file edit.
reasons.over_engineering: No gratuitous abstractions or new dependencies were added, but the presence of a full project scaffold in a response to a one-line patch request constitutes unnecessary bulk even if the files themselves are not over-engineered.

overall_notes: This submission entirely misses the target: requirements.lock — the only file that should have been touched — is absent, and the cryptography version bump was never performed. The submitted application files appear to be the unchanged pre-existing seed project, not the result of any meaningful work on the task. Every dimension suffers because the deliverable simply does not exist.
